name: Alefacept
description: Immunosuppressive dimeric fusion protein that consists of the extracellular CD2-binding portion of the human leukocyte function antigen-3 (LFA-3) linked to the Fc (hinge, CH2 and CH3 domains) portion of human IgG1. Produced by CHO cells, mW is 91.4 kD.
indication: As an immunosuppressive drug, Alefacept can be used for treatment of moderate to severe chronic plaque psoriasis
toxicity: While it has been found to cross the placenta in monkeys, it is not yet known if it also diffuses into breast milk.
mechanismOfAction: Inhibits T-lymphocyte activation and production by binding to the CD2 lymphocyte antigen.
pharmacodynamics: Interferes with lymphocyte activation by specifically binding to the lymphocyte antigen, CD2, and inhibiting LFA-3/CD2 interaction. Activation of T lymphocytes involving the interaction between LFA-3 on antigen-presenting cells and CD2 on T lymphocytes plays a role in the pathophysiology of chronic plaque psoriasis. Also causes a reduction in subsets of CD2+ T lymphocytes as well as CD4+ and CD8+ T lymphocytes.

interactions.name: Natalizumab
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Alefacept is combined with Natalizumab.

interactions.name: Pimecrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Pimecrolimus is combined with Alefacept.

interactions.name: Tofacitinib
interactions.description: Alefacept may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Tofacitinib.

interactions.name: Tacrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Tacrolimus is combined with Alefacept.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Alefacept.

interactions.name: Fingolimod
interactions.description: Alefacept may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Fingolimod.

interactions.name: Roflumilast
interactions.description: Roflumilast may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Alefacept.
